1: But Solomon was building his own house thirteen years, and he finished all his house.
2: He built also the house of the forest of Lebanon; the length thereof was was an hundred cubits, and the breadth thereof fifty cubits, and the height thereof thirty cubits, upon four rows of cedar pillars, with cedar beams upon the pillars.
3: And it was it was covered with cedar above upon the beams, that lay lay on forty five pillars, fifteen in in a row.
4: And there were there were windows in in three rows, and light was was against light in in three ranks.
5: And all the doors and posts were were square, with the windows: and light was was against light in in three ranks.
6: And he made a porch of pillars; the length thereof was was fifty cubits, and the breadth thereof thirty cubits: and the porch was was before them: and the other other pillars and the thick beam were were before them.
7: Then he made a porch for the throne where he might judge, even even the porch of judgment: and it was it was covered with cedar from one side of the floor to the other.
8: And his house where he dwelt had had another court within the porch, which which was of the like work. Solomon made also an house for Pharaoh's daughter, whom he had taken to wife to wife , like unto this porch.
9: All these were of were of costly stones, according to the measures of hewed stones, sawed with saws, within and without, even from the foundation unto the coping, and so so on the outside toward the great court.
10: And the foundation was of was of costly stones, even even great stones, stones of ten cubits, and stones of eight cubits.
11: And above were were costly stones, after the measures of hewed stones, and cedars.
12: And the great court round about was was with three rows of hewed stones, and a row of cedar beams, both for the inner court of the house of the LORD LORD , and for the porch of the house.
13: And king Solomon sent and fetched Hiram out of Tyre.
14: He was was a widow's son of the tribe of Naphtali, and his father was was a man of Tyre, a worker in brass: and he was filled with wisdom, and understanding, and cunning to work all works in brass. And he came to king Solomon, and wrought all his work.
15: For he cast two pillars of brass, of eighteen cubits high apiece: and a line of twelve cubits did compass either of them about.
16: And he made two chapiters of of molten brass, to set upon the tops of the pillars: the height of the one chapiter was was five cubits, and the height of the other chapiter was was five cubits:
17: and and nets of checker work, and wreaths of chain work, for the chapiters which were were upon the top of the pillars; seven for the one chapiter, and seven for the other chapiter.
18: And he made the pillars, and two rows round about upon the one network, to cover the chapiters that were were upon the top, with pomegranates: and so did he for the other chapiter.
19: And the chapiters that were were upon the top of the pillars were were of lily work in the porch, four cubits.
20: And the chapiters upon the two pillars had pomegranates had pomegranates also above, over against the belly which was was by the network: and the pomegranates were were two hundred in rows round about upon the other chapiter.
21: And he set up the pillars in the porch of the temple: and he set up the right pillar, and called the name thereof Jachin: and he set up the left pillar, and called the name thereof Boaz.
22: And upon the top of the pillars was was lily work: so was the work of the pillars finished.
23: And he made a molten sea, ten cubits from the one brim to the other: it was it was round all about, and his height was was five cubits: and a line of thirty cubits did compass it round about.
24: And under the brim of it round about there were there were knops compassing it, ten in a cubit, compassing the sea round about: the knops were were cast in two rows, when it was cast.
25: It stood upon twelve oxen, three looking toward the north, and three looking toward the west, and three looking toward the south, and three looking toward the east: and the sea was set was set above upon them, and all their hinder parts were were inward.
26: And it was was an hand breadth thick, and the brim thereof was wrought like the brim of a cup, with flowers of lilies: it contained two thousand baths.
27: And he made ten bases of brass; four cubits was was the length of one base, and four cubits the breadth thereof, and three cubits the height of it.
28: And the work of the bases was was on this manner: manner: they had borders, and the borders were were between the ledges:
29: and on the borders that were were between the ledges were were lions, oxen, and cherubims: and upon the ledges there was there was a base above: and beneath the lions and oxen were were certain additions made of thin work.
30: And every base had four brasen wheels, and plates of brass: and the four corners thereof had undersetters: under the laver were were undersetters molten, at the side of every addition.
31: And the mouth of it within the chapiter and above was was a cubit: but the mouth thereof was was round after after the work of the base, a cubit and an half: and also upon the mouth of it were were gravings with their borders, foursquare, not round.
32: And under the borders were were four wheels; and the axletrees of the wheels were joined were joined to the base: and the height of a wheel was was a cubit and half a cubit.
33: And the work of the wheels was was like the work of a chariot wheel: their axletrees, and their naves, and their felloes, and their spokes, were were all molten.
34: And there were there were four undersetters to the four corners of one base: and and the undersetters were were of the very base itself.
35: And in the top of the base was there was there a round compass of half a cubit high: and on the top of the base the ledges thereof and the borders thereof were were of the same.
36: For on the plates of the ledges thereof, and on the borders thereof, he graved cherubims, lions, and palm trees, according to the proportion of every one, and additions round about.
37: After this manner manner he made the ten bases: all of them had one casting, one measure, and and one size.
38: Then made he ten lavers of brass: one laver contained forty baths: and and every laver was four cubits: and and upon every one of the ten bases one laver.
39: And he put five bases on the right side of the house, and five on the left side of the house: and he set the sea on the right side of the house eastward over against the south.
40: And Hiram made the lavers, and the shovels, and the basons. So Hiram made an end of doing all the work that he made king Solomon for the house of the LORD LORD :
41: the two pillars, and the two two bowls of the chapiters that were were on the top of the two pillars; and the two networks, to cover the two bowls of the chapiters which were were upon the top of the pillars;
42: and four hundred pomegranates for the two networks, even even two rows of pomegranates for one network, to cover the two bowls of the chapiters that were were upon the pillars;
43: and the ten bases, and ten lavers on the bases;
44: and one sea, and twelve oxen under the sea;
45: and the pots, and the shovels, and the basons: and all these vessels, which Hiram made to king Solomon for the house of the LORD LORD , were of were of bright brass.
46: In the plain of Jordan did the king cast them, in the clay ground between Succoth and Zarthan.
47: And Solomon left all the vessels unweighed unweighed , because they were exceeding many: neither was the weight of the brass found out.
48: And Solomon made all the vessels that pertained pertained unto the house of the LORD LORD : the altar of gold, and the table of gold, whereupon the shewbread was was ,
49: and the candlesticks of pure gold, five on the right side side , and five on the left, before the oracle, with the flowers, and the lamps, and the tongs of of gold,
50: and the bowls, and the snuffers, and the basons, and the spoons, and the censers of of pure gold; and the hinges of of gold, both both for the doors of the inner house, the most holy place, and place, and for the doors of the house, to wit to wit , of the temple.
51: So was ended all the work that king Solomon made for the house of the LORD LORD . And Solomon brought in the things which David his father had dedicated; even even the silver, and the gold, and the vessels, did he put among the treasures of the house of the LORD LORD .
